tools.name: search_dog_problems
tools.description: Search the Dog Leadership Academy library of ~70 common dog behaviour problems. Returns matching articles with title, one-line description, TL;DR summary, article URL, and the CTA URL to the free 4-minute behavioural assessment. Use for questions like "my dog pulls on the leash", "reactive dog", "pu

tools.name: list_dog_problem_topics
tools.description: Return the taxonomy of the DLA library: six clusters (Reactivity, Loose-leash walking, Puppy foundations, Obedience/recall, Household behaviour problems, Method & philosophy) and the article titles in each. Use to enumerate what the library covers.

tools.name: get_dog_problem_article
tools.description: Return the TL;DR, section overview, and CTA URL for a single article from the DLA library, by its slug. Use after `search_dog_problems` finds a match. Does NOT return the exact protocol (that lives inside the paid programs) — only the diagnostic content and the CTA to the free assessment.

tools.name: get_program_info
tools.description: Return information about Dog Leadership Academy's coaching programs — pricing, what's included, and the DLA sales pitch. Use when the user asks how much it costs, what they get, or how DLA works. Options: essentials_monthly, essentials_annual, premium_monthly, premium_annual, or omit `tier` for all

tools.name: submit_dog_behaviour_assessment
tools.description: Submit the 8-question free behavioural assessment on the user's behalf. This is the same form that lives at https://www.dogleadershipacademy.com/assessment. On success we send a personalised written analysis to the owner's email within a few minutes, written by George Tran and the DLA team. The user

tools.name: create_enrollment_checkout
tools.description: Create a Stripe Checkout URL for the user to complete enrollment in a chosen tier. Returns a URL the user opens in a browser to pay — the agent NEVER handles payment or card details directly. Only call after the user has explicitly chosen a tier and asked to enroll.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_dog_problems, get_dog_problem_article · Public sink: submit_dog_behaviour_assessment.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_dog_problems, get_dog_problem_article · Public sink: submit_dog_behaviour_assessment

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
